intro: Breynia diospyrifolia, commonly known as Breynia calcarea or Kaludi, is a plant species belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae, characterized by its unique, umbrella-shaped inflorescences and edible fruits. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Breynia calcarea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
